ccs_lab.name: OpenEvo CCS Lab
ccs_lab.intro: Our earlier work treated evolutionary and behavioral science as an interdisciplinary lens for curriculum design — causal mapping, cooperation across textbooks, the Community Science Lab model. The OpenEvo CCS Lab extends that same instinct computationally: we represent curricula themselves — standards, learning progressions, competency frameworks — as structured, comparable knowledge graphs, so researchers and policymakers can see where interdisciplinary concepts cohere across grade bands and subjects, and where they don't.

flagship.name: OpenEvo ConceptBase (OECB)
flagship.description: The semantic backbone of the lab: a shared, version-controlled ontology and controlled vocabularies (profiled against SKOS, IEEE LOM, schema.org, and 1EdTech CASE) that let independently maintained curriculum models describe their content in a common, interoperable language — without imposing a single 'correct' curriculum.

apps.name: ConceptBase Explorer
apps.description: Interactive app for browsing the knowledge graph — no setup required. See how a single concept is defined across disciplines, and trace alignments across grade bands.

apps.name: eva_buch Explorer
apps.description: Interactive topic-modelling browser for German curriculum documents, supporting Dr. Susan Hanisch's forthcoming (2027) book on teaching evolutionary anthropology in schools.

featured_repos.blurb: Ontology, vocabularies, and schemas for the whole ecosystem — the OECB core.
A reference K–12 learning progression model spanning biology, social studies, and computer science.
A reference K–12 learning progression model for evolutionary biology.
German-language topic-modelling dataset and visualizations on evolutionary anthropology in curricula.
